questionText: What are ACID properties in DBMS? (Variant 1)
suggestedAnswer: ACID stands for Atomicity (all or nothing), Consistency (valid state), Isolation (independent transactions), and Durability (permanent write).

questionText: What is normalization and what is 3NF? (Variant 2)
suggestedAnswer: Normalization is organizing data to reduce redundancy. 3NF requires that the relation is in 2NF and has no transitive dependencies on the primary key.

questionText: What is the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases? (Variant 3)
suggestedAnswer: SQL is relational, schema-based, and scales vertically. NoSQL is non-relational, schema-less (document, key-value, graph), and scales horizontally.

questionText: Explain Joins in SQL. (Variant 4)
suggestedAnswer: Joins combine records from two or more tables based on a related column. Types include Inner, Left (Outer), Right (Outer), and Full Joins.

questionText: What is indexing and why is it used? (Variant 5)
suggestedAnswer: Indexing is a data structure technique used to quickly locate and access data in a database without searching every row, typically structured as B-Trees.
